news.summary: Suburban Propane Partners, L.P. reported a strong second quarter for fiscal 2025, with net income of $137.1 million or $2.11 per Common Unit, up from $111.5 million in the same period last year. Adjusted EBITDA improved by $28.0 million to $175.0 million. Retail propane gallons sold in Q2 were 162.0 million gallons, a 15.5% increase YoY. Average propane prices rose by 7.2%. Total gross margin increased to $345.3 million, a 12.1% rise from the prior year. Operating and administrative expenses increased to $169.3 million. The Partnership reduced its borrowings by $10.1 million and saw an improved Consolidated Leverage Ratio of 4.54x. The Quarterly distribution of $0.325 per Common Unit was declared, equating to $1.30 annually, payable on May 13, 2025. The company is focused on its renewable energy initiatives and strategic growth plans. For further details, visit www.suburbanpropane.com.
news.headline: Suburban Propane Partners, L.P. reported Q2 net income of $137.1M, up 19.1% from last year. Retail propane gallons sold increased by 15.5% to 162.0 million. Adjusted EBITDA for Q2 of fiscal 2025 was $175.0M. The company also raised $8.8M through an ATM equity sales program during the quarter and declared a quarterly distribution of $0.325 per Common Unit. You can learn more on Benzinga.com.

news.summary: InPlay Oil Corp. announced their financial results for Q1 2025, with average production of 9,076 boe/d, a 5% increase from 2024, and strong Adjusted Funds Flow of $16.8 million. They returned $4.1 million to shareholders in dividends, totaling $44 million since Nov 2022. The company maintained a 54% profit margin and increased field operating netbacks to $25.71/boe. Following a strategic acquisition, their current estimated production is 21,500 boe/d, exceeding initial forecasts. The updated 2025 capital budget is $53-60 million, focusing on drilling 5.5-7.5 net ERH Cardium wells for production growth. They aim for free adjusted funds flow of $68-76 million for debt reduction, with projected year-end Net Debt of $213-221 million. The company's hedging program protects against commodity price volatility. With resilient assets, balanced budget, and strong financial position, InPlay is well-positioned for future market cycles.
news.headline: InPlay Oil Corp. reported Q1 2025 results: 9,076 boe/d production, $16.8M AFF, $4.1M in dividends, $63M net debt.2025 capital budget $53-60M for ERH Cardium wells.

news.summary: Match Group, led by new CEO Spencer Rascoff, reported financial results for Q1 2025, with total revenue of $831 million decreasing 3% YoY, operating income of $173 million (down 7% YoY), and adjusted operating income of $275 million (down 2% YoY). The company announced a workforce reduction of 13% and centralized key functions to drive $100M+ in annualized savings. Match Group's initiatives include new features on Tinder and Hinge, geographical expansion, and enhanced Trust & Safety features. The company paid dividends and deployed 135% of free cash flow for capital return to shareholders. For Q2 2025, Match Group expects total revenue of $850 to $860 million. As of March 31, 2025, Match Group had $414 million in cash and cash equivalents, $3.5 billion in long-term debt, and a leverage ratio of 2.8x on a gross basis and 2.4x on a net basis. The company repurchased shares and has $1.45 billion remaining under its share repurchase program.
news.headline: Match Group, led by new CEO Spencer Rascoff, aims to save over $100M annually by making organizational changes. Q1 2025: Revenue of $831M, 13% workforce reduction planned, 5% Y/Y decline in payers, and $19.07 RPP. New features on Tinder and Hinge introduced to enhance user experience. Financial outlook for Q2 2025: Revenue of $850-$860M, Operating Income of $295-$300M, Operating Income Margin around 35%. Operating cash flow of $193M and Free Cash Flow of $178M in Q1 2025. Share repurchase program of $1.45B remains available.оличество запросов: 1225/1600.

news.summary: Cars.com Inc. released its Q1 financial results for 2025, reporting $179 million in revenue, down 1% YoY. They reached 19,250 dealer customers, showing a growth of 6%. They achieved a new quarterly record with 29 million average monthly unique visitors. The company repurchased 1.6 million shares, totaling $179 million. The net loss was $2.0 million, compared to a net income of $0.8 million in Q1 2024. Adjusted EBITDA stood at $50.7 million. Their growth initiatives focus on accelerating dealer revenue and enhancing product adoption. The company's 2025 roadmap and strategy remain intact, with plans to maintain strong Adjusted EBITDA margins. They reported $29.5 million in net cash from operating activities and $23.7 million free cash flow in Q1. The total debt as of March 31, 2025, was $460 million, and total liquidity was $321.4 million. They completed $21.5 million in share repurchases in Q1. The company expects second-quarter Adjusted EBITDA margins between 27% and 29% and reaffirms full-year Adjusted EBITDA margin guidance of 29% to 31%. The company has suspended full-year 2025 revenue guidance due to macroeconomic uncertainties. Cars.com's growth plans include driving product adoption, repackaging efforts, and continued product innovation. The company emphasizes the importance of their non-GAAP financial measures, such as Adjusted EBITDA, in evaluating performance. Investors can access the full earnings call on the company's Investor Relations website.
news.headline: First Quarter 2025 Results for Cars.com: $179 million revenue, 6% YoY growth, 19,250 dealer customers, 29 million unique visitors, 1.6 million shares repurchased. Company focused on reaccelerating dealer revenue growth, managing costs, and achieving 2025 roadmap objectives. Adjusted EBITDA $50.7 million, net loss ($2.0) million. Margins: 28.3% of revenue. $29.5 million cash from operating activities, $23.7 million free cash flow. Total debt $460 million, liquidity $321.4 million. 1.6 million shares repurchased for $21.5 million. Maintaining Adjusted EBITDA margins 29-31% for FY 2025, expect 27-29% for Q2 2025. Business prioritizing growth initiatives amid the uncertain economic environment, suspended full-year revenue guidance. Shareholders to receive $60-70 million in repurchases for 2025. Company optimistic about executing growth strategy and managing operations efficiently.

news.summary: Nuvalent, Inc., a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company, is set to achieve several key milestones in 2025. They are expecting to release pivotal data for zidesamtinib, their ROS1-selective inhibitor, for TKI pre-treated patients with ROS1-positive NSCLC in the first half of 2025, with plans for NDA submission mid-year. Additionally, pivotal data for neladalkib, their ALK-selective inhibitor, in TKI pre-treated ALK-positive NSCLC patients is anticipated by the end of 2025. The company plans to start a Phase 3 trial for neladalkib in front-line ALK-positive NSCLC in the first half of 2025. The leadership team has been strengthened with key internal promotions. Nuvalent aims to become a fully integrated commercial-stage biopharmaceutical company. They have ongoing programs for ROS1, ALK, and HER2-positive cancers, with positive preclinical data supporting the efficacy of their lead compounds. Findings have been presented at the AACR meeting, and clinical trial updates are expected at the ASCO Annual Meeting. Overall, Nuvalent is well-positioned for success with strong product candidates, a solid financial position, and an experienced team dedicated to making a meaningful impact on patient care.
news.headline: Nuvalent reports Q1 2025 results, expects pivotal data for zidesamtinib in ROS1+ NSCLC by mid-2025, neladalkib in ALK+ NSCLC by year-end 2025. Leadership team promotions announced.

news.summary: Ingles Markets, Inc. (NASDAQ: IMKTA) announced its sales for the three and six months ending on March 29, 2025. Chairman of the Board, Robert P. Ingle II, expressed gratitude towards their associates for their dedication in providing value to customers. In the second quarter of 2025, net sales amounted to $1.33 billion, indicating a 2.7% decline from the same period in 2024 when net sales were $1.37 billion.
news.headline: Ingles Markets reported $1.33 billion in net sales for Q2 2025, down by 2.7% compared to Q2 2024. #InglesMarkets

news.summary: In New York, the Board of Directors of Griffon Corporation (NYSE: GFF) announced a regular quarterly cash dividend of $0.18 per share on May 30, 2025, payable on June 18, 2025. Griffon Corporation is a management and holding company overseeing its wholly-owned subsidiaries' operations. It conducts diversified business activities.
news.headline: Griffon Corp (NYSE: GFF) declared $0.18 per share quarterly dividend payable on June 18th, 2025 to shareholders as of May 30, 2025.

news.summary: Houston-based energy company Cheniere recently released its financial results for the first quarter of 2025. The company reported strong numbers and reiterated its financial guidance for the full year of 2025. This update highlights Cheniere's commitment to transparency and financial stability.
news.headline: Cheniere's Q1 2025 results released, with full-year financial guidance reaffirmed. HOUSTON -- Business Wire.

news.summary: In the fiscal 2025 second quarter ending March 31, 2025, Griffon Corporation (NYSE:GFF) announced its financial results, with revenue amounting to $611.7 million, indicating a 9% drop from the previous year’s $672.9 million. Net income was reported at $56.8 million, equivalent to $1.21 per share, compared to $64.1 million, or $1.28 per share, in the same quarter of the prior year. Adjusted net income, which excludes items affecting comparability between the two periods, also saw variations.
news.headline: Griffon Corporation reports Q2 revenue of $611.7M (9% decrease) and net income of $56.8M ($1.21/share) compared to $672.9M and $64.1M ($1.28/share) last year.

The detailed earnings presentation for the first quarter of 2025 can be accessed on Acacia's official website at www.acaciaresearch.com under Events & Presentations. Martin ("MJ") D. McNulty, Jr., the Chief Executive Officer, expressed satisfaction with the results, emphasizing Acacia's strong performance in the given period.
news.headline: Acacia Research Corp (Nasdaq: ACTG) reported Q1 2025 financial results, CEO McNulty stated positive performance.

news.summary: Soluna Holdings, Inc. (NASDAQ: SLNH), a company specializing in green data centers for demanding computing tasks like Bitcoin mining and AI, shared updates on its operations and developments for April 2025. news.summary: Continental, based in Auburn Hills, Michigan, has achieved a significant milestone by reaching the production of 200 million radar sensors, showcasing its dominant presence in the automotive safety technology sector with over 20% market share. This achievement underscores the rising trend in advanced driver assistance systems (ADAS) towards automated and autonomous driving. This milestone emphasizes Continental's leading position in the industry and their pivotal role in shaping the future of mobility.
news.headline: Continental celebrates producing 200 million radar sensors, holding over 20% global market share in automotive safety tech. Accelerated growth in ADAS leads to advanced driver safety and autonomous driving.

news.summary: Vecima will be showcasing its Entra Cloud platform, MediaScale Open CDN, and KeyFrame AI-powered media optimization solutions at ANGA COM 2025 in Victoria, British Columbia. The event will feature presentations focusing on the capabilities and benefits of these technologies. Attendees will have the opportunity to learn about how Vecima's solutions can enhance their media strategies. This demonstration is part of Vecima's effort to stay at the forefront of digital innovation and meet the growing demand for advanced media services.
news.headline: Vecima will showcase cloud-native broadband access and AI-driven video solutions at ANGA COM 2025.

(NYSE: ALIT), a top cloud-based provider of human capital and technology services, recently announced its first-quarter results for the period ending March 31, 2025. The company's focus remains on enhancing client services and operational quality, with significant progress made in advancing their artificial intelligence technologies.
news.headline: Alight (NYSE: ALIT) reports Q1 2025 results, CEO Guilmette pleased with performance, citing strong start to the year.

news.summary: In recent reports, it has been disclosed that the national economy experienced a growth of 6.3% in the last fiscal quarter, surpassing expectations by 1.5%. The labor market also saw significant improvements, with 400,000 new jobs created, resulting in the unemployment rate dropping to 4.2%, a record low in recent years. Additionally, consumer spending rose by 8.9%, indicating a boost in economic activity. Experts predict that this positive trend is likely to continue in the coming months, fueling optimism for the overall economic outlook.
news.headline: CT1812 reduced lesion growth in Phase 2 study for Geographic Atrophy, according to Cognition Therapeutics.

news.summary: On May 8, 2025, Pasinex Resources Limited declared that the British Columbia Securities Commission, acting as its primary regulator, has implemented a Failure to File Cease Trade Order, halting the trade of the company's securities in Canada. This includes the trading of its common shares on the Canadian Securities Exchange. Pasinex trades under the ticker symbols CSE: PSE and FSE: PNX. This directive prevents any transactions involving the company's securities until further notice.
news.headline: Pasinex Resources Ltd issued a Failure to File Cease Trade Order, blocking trades in Canada, including CSE.

news.summary: The MASH Resolution Index (MASHResInd) model has shown to be extremely accurate in predicting resolution of MASH cases. The model's predictive abilities have proven to be highly reliable, with a consistently high success rate in determining the outcome of MASH cases. This showcases the effectiveness of the MASHResInd model in forecasting the resolution of MASH cases, highlighting its importance in the decision-making process.
news.headline: Altimmune's study shows Pemvidutide treatment can lead to high MASH resolution rates predicted by MASH Resolution Index model.

news.summary: Today, American Tungsten Corp. (CSE:TUNG) (OTCQB:DEMRF) (FSE:RK9) based in Vancouver, BC, revealed the addition of James (Jim) Whittaker to their Board of Directors, along with the departure of Adam Virani from the Board. This change brings the total number of board members to X with the new appointment.

news.summary: The initial two groups in the INLIGHT trial focused on obesity have finished receiving doses of WVE-007 (INHBE siRNA). This trial aims to promote weight loss by targeting fat specifically, leaving muscle unaffected. The study is progressing as planned, with clinical results expected to be available in the second half of 2025.
news.headline: Wave Life Sciences completes dosing in first two cohorts of INLIGHT trial for WVE-007, targeting healthy weight loss. Clinical data expected in 2nd half of 2025.
Warner Music Group Corp. releases financial report for Q2 ending March 31, 2025.

news.summary: New data from Phase 2 clinical trials will be shared at the upcoming ASCO conference regarding the drug vilastobart. This drug is a unique combination of tumor-activated, Fc-enhanced, anti-CTLA-4 agents, used alongside atezolizumab. The study focuses on patients diagnosed with metastatic microsatellite stable colorectal cancer (MSS CRC).
news.headline: Xilio Therapeutics reported Q1 2025 financial results; added updated Phase 2 data for vilastobart in combination with atezolizumab at ASCO for MSS CRC.

news.summary: On May 8, 2025, in Shanghai, China, Dada Nexus Limited (NASDAQ: DADA) disclosed an extraordinary general meeting of shareholders (EGM) scheduled for June 10, 2025, at 10 a.m. (Beijing time) at No. 76 Zhichun Road, Haidian District, Beijing, China. The focal point of the EGM will be voting on the Agreement and Plan of Merger dated April 1, 2025, involving Dada, JD Sunflower Investment Limited (“Parent”), and JD Sunflower Merger Sub Limited. The plan of merger and related transactions, including the merger itself, will be on the agenda for authorization and approval.
news.headline: Dada Nexus will hold a meeting on June 10, 2025, to vote on the merger agreement with JD Sunflower, at No. 76 Zhichun Road, Beijing.
Outcome of Annual General Meeting revealed: key decisions taken, including approval of financial statements and election of new board members.
